intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-compliant with the visual evidence. The pots are present, but the biological occupants are either absent, dormant, or have suffered total systemic loss.

inventory_reconciliation.P1 (Yellow Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; soil appears barren.
inventory_reconciliation.P2 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.P3 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent or completely withered.
inventory_reconciliation.P4 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface, no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit figurine remains, but no foliage detected.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface, no signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dry and compacted across all units.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of a spoon in T-3 and a cylindrical object (likely a sensor or marker) in T-2, indicating human intervention/maintenance attempts.

temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Introduction of a metal spoon into the biome; no biological growth observed.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Removal of spoon; introduction of a cylindrical white object (marker/sensor) into the yellow pot (P1).
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Removal of all external objects; return to a state of total biological vacancy.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: All specimens are confirmed dead or missing. The biome is currently non-functional.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of non-botanical objects (spoon, sensor) suggests an attempt to monitor or intervene in a failing system, but the biological components have failed to survive.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete collapse of the botanical biome. Across the five-day observation window, the pots have transitioned from potentially dormant to clearly empty. The presence of a spoon and a sensor indicates an active attempt to manage the environment, but these interventions have not resulted in any visible plant recovery. The environment is currently a sterile, non-productive setup.
